rationale: The "satisfying hands dragging puzzle pieces" hook taps into the same casual 25-55 puzzler audience and shares a drag-and-drop tactile language, so the audience overlap is solid. However, the visual frictions are significant: jigsaw pieces are not polyominoes, the white/red/blue member palette clashes with Block Blast's signature royal-blue + dark-navy + gold board, and there's no 8x8 grid or line-clear payoff in the centroid hook. Recommend testing only if reshot with actual Block Blast gameplay (real grid, branded palette, line-clear satisfaction beat) using the "hands-on-phone" framing as a wrapper—otherwise the creative will mislead users and tank IPM. Also note the thin cluster evidence (1 creative, 1 advertiser, 117d old) means this is a low-confidence signal, not a proven winner.
